_note: Miami HQ data — Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens.

venue.name: Hard Rock Stadium
venue.address: 347 Don Shula Dr, Miami Gardens, FL 33056
venue.notes: Clear bag policy strictly enforced. No re-entry once inside. Gates open 2.5 hours before kickoff.
venue.parkingNote: On-site parking available from $50. Book in advance via SeatGeek or the official FIFA app. Lots fill 3+ hours early.

transit.recommended: Rideshare (Uber/Lyft) or personal vehicle — stadium is in Miami Gardens, ~16 miles from Downtown Miami
lines: No direct rail. Miami-Dade Transit Route 27 stops near the stadium.
tips: Rideshare is the most practical option. Designate a pickup zone before the match ends.
Metrorail + bus combo from Dadeland South: 45–60 min. Not ideal for large groups.
Parking lots on Stephens Island and the east side fill earliest — arrive 2+ hours early if driving.
Wynwood and Brickell are 30+ min away by car on match days — head out early.
transit.parking: On-site parking $50–$80. Reserve via FIFA official app or SeatGeek. Cash lots available but limited.
transit.rideshare: Designated Uber/Lyft pickup on the stadium's east side, Gate H area. Expect surge pricing post-match.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Bayfront Park
fanZones.notes: Free entry. Massive main stage, food vendors, live music, giant screens. Best pre-match energy in the city.

fanZones.name: Wynwood Fan Zone
fanZones.notes: Outdoor watch party in the arts district. Multiple screens, food trucks. Check local listings for confirmation.

preGame.name: Wynwood Walls area bars
preGame.notes: Best pre-game energy in Miami. Bars along NW 2nd Ave pack out hours before kickoff. International fan mix.

preGame.name: Brickell City Centre
preGame.notes: Upscale pre-game option. Pull & Pour, Sugar, and Silverbar attract a soccer crowd on match days.

preGame.name: Coconut Grove
preGame.notes: Relaxed waterfront vibe. Good for earlier arrivals who want to avoid peak-hour traffic heading north.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Morocco vs Haiti — passionate North African and Caribbean fans. Miami's diverse fan base will be in full voice. Afternoon kickoff.
Sweden vs Tunisia — European and North African supporters. Solid atmosphere but smaller traveling fan bases.
Scotland vs Morocco — Group C stakes. Scotland fans travel in force. Miami's Scottish community shows up.
France knockout match — Les Bleus at Hard Rock Stadium. Miami's French and Francophone community + massive Brazil of the north vibes.

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Morocco vs Haiti — afternoon match. I-95 and 836 will be congested. Leave early or plan rideshare.
tips: Leave Miami proper 2 hours before kickoff
Rideshare surge pricing likely at post-match exit — walk to secondary pickup zone east of stadium
Parking is plentiful but expensive; reserve in advance

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Sweden vs Tunisia — smaller crowd, more manageable traffic. Still plan for 30-min extra travel time.
tips: Most manageable match-day traffic of Miami's group stage
Good day to test your route if you're attending multiple matches
I-95 express lanes help heading back south

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Scotland vs Morocco — evening match draws big crowds. Stadium at capacity. I-95 northbound a slog.
tips: Take Turnpike instead of I-95 if heading from South Miami
Scottish fans arrive in full kit — atmosphere builds from 3pm
Post-match rideshare surge: walk toward the Dolphin Expressway exit area

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: France knockout match — Miami's biggest WC moment. City-wide energy, maximum traffic on all corridors.
tips: Plan to be in your seat 90 minutes early — this is a major event
Book post-match restaurant or ride in advance
Monitor Waze live — alternate routes via 826 or US-1 may be faster

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Morocco vs Haiti at Hard Rock — atmospheric match with strong local fan energy. Miami's Haitian and North African communities will create something special.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Sweden vs Tunisia — good football, manageable crowds, best ticket value of Miami's group stage. Great introduction to the tournament.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Scotland vs Morocco with Group C stakes — could be a must-win. Scottish fans are legendary travelers. Miami's most complete match-day atmosphere of the group stage.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: France in a knockout match at Hard Rock. Les Bleus on the biggest stage with elimination on the line. Miami's defining World Cup moment.
